scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced a recommendation-only package grounded in PMPs, RFIs, revised estimates, schedule updates, permit posture, and closeout effects without overstating authority.
The specialist blocked deterministic planning until the current RFI, estimate basis, drawing revision, schedule baseline, and PMIS record links were supplied.
Capacity shortfall across design, estimating, and site-review resources was quantified with deferrals and staged review options rather than unsupported commitments.
Competing code, client, and closeout priorities remained explicit and were escalated because no approved tie-break rule existed.
Requests for final change-order approval, permit action, and execution readiness were refused and routed to the correct approval owners.
The lane stayed industry-specific by using named A and E artifacts such as RFIs, ASIs, PCOs, revised SCEs, PMIS states, permit records, and punch-list impacts.
Missing field shutdown, special-inspection, and engineer-review facts correctly blocked sequencing and produced a site-readiness escalation.
The lane refused to treat the industry label as cosmetic and rejected generic organizational change work that lacked project artifacts and site dependencies.
Code and sealing questions stayed outside the lane and were routed to licensed design or permitting ownership while preserving only planning impact.
Conflicting PMIS, project-history, and AHJ records were escalated instead of being synthesized into unsupported readiness.
Tenant-specific review boards, change classes, approval thresholds, and amendment triggers were incorporated without losing public-baseline boundary discipline.
Late change impacts on punch-list, as-builts, training, occupancy, and closeout were surfaced without implying final completion or acceptance authority.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all planning-scheduling-and-allocation abilities, and the required cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
